user.title: Set up Roadplanner
user.description: Uses the existing Roadbook and review inbox. The optional native assistant reads current trip data directly from Home Assistant and never applies changes automatically.

data.non_admin_role: Default role for authenticated non-admin users
data.assistant_max_history: Conversation messages per trip and user
data.onedrive_client_id: Public Application (client) ID of a Microsoft Entra app for personal Microsoft accounts and device-code sign-in. This is not an API key and no client secret is used. It can also be entered later under Roadplanner → Photos → Set up OneDrive.
data.onedrive_auto_assign: Automatically assign photos by date and GPS
data.onedrive_date_buffer_days: Buffer before and after the trip (days)

data_description.roadbook_path: Path relative to /config. Default: www/roadbook. active_trip.json remains the active-trip pointer.
data_description.handoff_path: Private folder outside www containing inbox, pending, processed, failed, archive, applied, and outbox directories.
data_description.backup_count: Maximum number of private trip snapshots.
data_description.refresh_interval: Seconds between checks for external edits. Reads never change route data.
data_description.enable_handoff_webhook: Enables the token-protected read-only context webhook and the fixed review-only POST endpoint /api/roadplanner/drive-import.
data_description.auto_scan_handoffs: Scan the private inbox folder at the refresh interval and import valid ChangeSet files automatically.
data_description.auto_apply_changesets: Apply ChangeSets only when they explicitly request automatic mode, contain no open questions, and match the active trip revision.
data_description.allow_destructive_auto_apply: Also permit remove_day and remove_stop during automatic application. Keep disabled until the workflow is proven.
data_description.non_admin_role: Controls Roadplanner panel access for all authenticated non-admin users: viewer can only read, editor can also edit routes, and approver can additionally process handoffs.
data_description.assistant_provider: Provider used by the native Roadplanner assistant. This release supports Gemini.
data_description.gemini_api_key: Stored in the Home Assistant config entry and used only by the backend. It is never sent to the browser. Leave blank in options to keep the existing key.
data_description.gemini_model: Model ID used for conversation and structured planning. Default: gemini-3.5-flash.
data_description.assistant_enable_research: Allows the assistant to use Gemini Google Search grounding for current recommendations. Search requests can be billable and send the relevant question and bounded trip context to Google.
data_description.assistant_max_history: Maximum number of recent volatile chat messages retained verbatim for each Home Assistant user and trip. Older messages are compacted into a local rolling summary instead of being silently dropped. The session is cleared on integration reload or Home Assistant restart.
data_description.geocoding_enabled: When Changes review is pressed, concrete new stops are resolved through OpenStreetMap Nominatim. Only place queries are sent; ambiguous results remain open questions.
data_description.geocoding_url: HTTPS URL of a Nominatim-compatible search endpoint. Default: the public OpenStreetMap Nominatim service. The provider can be changed without a software update.
data_description.gemini_fallback_model: Optional model used after the primary model remains unavailable following transient-error retries. Leave blank to disable fallback. Default: gemini-2.5-flash.
data_description.assistant_request_timeout: Overall time budget for one Gemini request including retries and fallback. Range: 20 to 180 seconds.
data_description.assistant_retry_attempts: Number of bounded retries for network errors, rate limits, timeouts, and Gemini 5xx responses before an optional fallback model is tried.
data_description.assistant_min_request_interval: Prevents API bursts by spacing Gemini requests on the server. Range: 0.5 to 15 seconds. Recommended: 2 seconds.
data_description.assistant_max_queue: Limits assistant requests waiting at the same time. Excess requests are rejected clearly instead of being sent to Gemini in parallel. Range: 1 to 50. Recommended: 8.
data_description.assistant_autonomy_level: answers only replies to questions; suggestions may proactively recommend options; change_basket additionally records clear user decisions for later review. No level can apply route changes automatically.
data_description.assistant_copilot_enabled: Adds a manual daily briefing button that summarizes today, tomorrow, risks, and useful next steps from the current Roadbook.
data_description.assistant_copilot_auto_briefing: When enabled, the assistant creates at most one briefing per local day when the assistant tab is opened. It never creates or applies route changes.
data_description.assistant_debug_enabled: Shows an admin-only diagnostics view with request IDs, sanitized provider health, durations, context scope, memory compaction, and plugin status. Prompts, trip content, and API keys are excluded.
data_description.routing_enabled: Enables manual road-distance and drive-time calculation. Routes are never recalculated on page load and are stored as derived data in the Roadbook.
data_description.routing_provider: Provider abstraction used for street routing. This release supports OSRM-compatible servers.
data_description.routing_url: HTTPS server root or path prefix. Roadplanner appends /route/v1/<profile>. No credentials, query, or fragment are allowed.
data_description.routing_profile: OSRM profile name such as driving or car, depending on the configured server.
data_description.routing_request_timeout: Time budget for one routing request. Range: 10 to 180 seconds.
data_description.routing_min_request_interval: Server-side spacing between route requests. Keep at least 1 second for public community servers.
data_description.archive_path: Private path inside /config and outside www. Stores original documents, confirmed expenses, and travel todos.
data_description.document_max_upload_mb: Maximum size of one upload. Range: 1 to 100 MB. Files larger than 10 MB can be stored but are not sent inline to Gemini for analysis.
data_description.document_analysis_enabled: Allows uploaded PDFs, images, and text files to be analyzed by the configured Gemini provider. Extracted data always requires user confirmation before it is stored.
data_description.default_currency: Three-letter ISO currency code such as EUR, PLN, or SEK. Exchange rates are never guessed automatically.
data_description.onedrive_enabled: Enables the read-only OneDrive Personal media plugin. Original photos remain in OneDrive.
data_description.onedrive_client_id: Public Application (client) ID of a Microsoft Entra app for personal Microsoft accounts and device-code sign-in. This is not an API key and no client secret is used. It can also be entered later under Roadplanner → Photos → Set up OneDrive.
data_description.onedrive_photo_folder: Path below the OneDrive root, for example Pictures/Camera Roll. Roadplanner only reads this folder.
data_description.onedrive_auto_sync: Checks the selected folder periodically for new and changed photos.
data_description.onedrive_sync_interval: Minutes between background delta checks. Range: 5 to 1440.
data_description.onedrive_auto_assign: Uses photo capture time and GPS metadata to suggest or automatically create stop/day album assignments.
data_description.onedrive_recursive: Traverses subfolders. Year folders outside the trip window and hidden thumbnail folders are skipped.
data_description.onedrive_date_buffer_days: Roadplanner includes photos this many days before the trip starts and after it ends. Range: 0 to 30 days.
data_description.onedrive_max_items_per_run: Limits metadata entries per synchronization run. Large archives continue automatically across multiple runs. Range: 100 to 5000.

error.invalid_path: At least one path is invalid. Paths must remain inside /config; private paths must not be below www.
error.invalid_geocoding_url: The geocoding URL must be a valid HTTPS endpoint without credentials, query, or fragment.
error.invalid_routing_url: The routing URL or profile is invalid. Use an HTTPS endpoint without credentials, query, or fragment.
error.invalid_currency: The default currency must be a three-letter ISO code such as EUR.
error.missing_onedrive_client_id: A public Microsoft application ID is required for OneDrive sign-in. You can also enter it later under Roadplanner → Photos → Set up OneDrive.

init.title: Roadplanner options
init.description: Changes reload the integration. Assistant chat is volatile; approved Roadbook data remains the only persistent memory.

get_trip_summary.name: Get trip summary
get_trip_summary.description: Return a compact summary and current revision.

get_days.name: Get travel days
get_days.description: Return a paginated list of travel days.

include_stops.name: Include stops
include_stops.description: Include compact stops in each returned day.

get_day.name: Get travel day
get_day.description: Return one day and paginated stops by day ID.

search_stops.name: Search stops
search_stops.description: Search stops and return stable day and stop IDs.

stop_type.name: Stop type
stop_type.description: Optional stop type filter or new type.

list_trips.name: List trips
list_trips.description: List trip directories known to Roadplanner.

set_active_trip.name: Set active trip
set_active_trip.description: Validate a trip and switch the active pointer.

expected_active_trip.name: Expected active trip
expected_active_trip.description: Optional guard against concurrent pointer changes.

update_trip.name: Update trip
update_trip.description: Update selected trip fields with revision protection.

patch.name: Patch
patch.description: Object containing only fields to change.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

add_day.name: Add travel day
add_day.description: Add one travel day with revision protection.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

start.name: Start
start.description: Start location or free-form start text.

end.name: End
end.description: End location or free-form destination text.

details.name: Details
details.description: Structured JSON object for extension data.

patch.name: Patch
patch.description: Object containing only fields to change.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

remove_day.name: Remove travel day
remove_day.description: Remove one day, optionally including its stops.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

remove_stops.name: Remove stops
remove_stops.description: Explicitly allow deleting stops with the day.

add_stop.name: Add stop
add_stop.description: Add one stop to an existing travel day.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

stop_type.name: Stop type
stop_type.description: Optional stop type filter or new type.

details.name: Details
details.description: Structured JSON object for extension data.

patch.name: Patch
patch.description: Object containing only fields to change.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

remove_stop.name: Remove stop
remove_stop.description: Remove one stop from a travel day.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

export_trip.name: Export trip
export_trip.description: Return a combined read-only JSON export.

create_backup.name: Create backup
create_backup.description: Create a private snapshot of the active trip.

reason.name: Reason
reason.description: Short reason stored in the backup manifest.

adopt_external_changes.name: Adopt external changes
adopt_external_changes.description: Validate and adopt intentional file edits.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Current revision read immediately before this write.

list_handoffs.name: List handoffs
list_handoffs.description: List pending, conflicting, and review-required ChangeSets.

get_handoff.name: Get handoff
get_handoff.description: Read a pending, applied, or archived ChangeSet handoff.

handoff_id.name: Handoff ID
handoff_id.description: Stable pending or archived handoff ID.

ingest_handoff.name: Ingest handoff
ingest_handoff.description: Place JSON or text containing a valid ChangeSet in the inbox.

content.name: Content
content.description: ChangeSet JSON, optionally inside a Markdown JSON code block.

content_type.name: Content type
content_type.description: MIME type of the handoff content.

external_id.name: External ID
external_id.description: Idempotency key supplied by the external bridge.

base_revision.name: Base revision
base_revision.description: Route revision used when the proposal was created.

metadata.name: Metadata
metadata.description: JSON object with non-canonical source metadata.

archive_handoff.name: Archive handoff
archive_handoff.description: Reject or defer a handoff that was not applied.

handoff_id.name: Handoff ID
handoff_id.description: Stable pending or archived handoff ID.

scan_handoffs.name: Scan handoff folder
scan_handoffs.description: Import valid ChangeSet files from the private inbox folder.

get_handoff_endpoint.name: Get handoff bridge credentials
get_handoff_endpoint.description: Returns the secret URL and header token for the external ChangeSet bridge.

export_context.name: Export route context
export_context.description: Refresh bounded JSON and Markdown context files.

ingest_changeset.name: Ingest ChangeSet
ingest_changeset.description: Validate a structured ChangeSet and place it in the inbox.

external_id.name: External ID
external_id.description: Idempotency key supplied by the source.

preview_handoff.name: Preview handoff
preview_handoff.description: Validate one ChangeSet against the current trip without writing.

apply_handoff.name: Apply handoff
apply_handoff.description: Apply all ChangeSet operations atomically in one revision.

expected_revision.name: Expected revision
expected_revision.description: Must equal the current and base revision.

confirm_destructive.name: Confirm deletions
confirm_destructive.description: Required for ChangeSets with remove operations.

get_handoff_folders.name: Get handoff folders
get_handoff_folders.description: Return the private inbox, processed, pending, applied, and failed paths.

rotate_handoff_secret.name: Rotate external bridge secret
rotate_handoff_secret.description: Invalidate the previous shared secret and return a new administrator-only secret.
